summary: Fix timing out TestHandlers_ListControls_WithPagination test in compliance-service. The 2-minute timeout with panic suggests blocking database operations without proper timeout context, potentially from lock contention or connection pool exhaustion during pagination queries.

user_stories.i_want: all compliance-service tests to pass consistently without timing out
user_stories.so_that: I can confidently make changes to the compliance service
acceptance_criteria: No test database connection leaks occur
Tests can run in parallel without blocking

user_stories.i_want: proper timeout handling in all database operations
user_stories.so_that: tests fail fast with clear error messages instead of hanging
acceptance_criteria: Timeout values are configurable via environment variable
Default timeout is 30 seconds for queries
Timeout errors are logged with context

user_stories.i_want: proper test cleanup to prevent resource exhaustion
user_stories.so_that: multiple tests can run without database lock issues
acceptance_criteria: setupHandlerTest properly cleans up database state
HTTP test server is closed after each test
Connection pool is not exhausted between tests

functional_requirements.title: Add context timeout to database operations
functional_requirements.description: All database operations in compliance-service must use a context with explicit timeout to prevent indefinite blocking. The handlers must wrap request contexts with appropriate timeouts.

functional_requirements.title: Add database query logging and debugging
functional_requirements.description: Add debug logging before and after database queries in ListControls to identify blocking operations. Log query execution time.

functional_requirements.title: Fix test setup cleanup
functional_requirements.description: Ensure setupHandlerTest properly truncates all compliance tables and closes resources to prevent state leakage between tests.

functional_requirements.title: Add connection pool configuration for tests
functional_requirements.description: Configure the connection pool with appropriate MaxConns and MinConns for test environment to prevent exhaustion.

functional_requirements.title: Add health check to test database setup
functional_requirements.description: Verify database connection is healthy before running each test to catch issues early.

functional_requirements.title: One-click compliance report generation
functional_requirements.description: Generate SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA compliance reports on demand

functional_requirements.title: 99.99% uptime SLA with monitoring dashboard
functional_requirements.description: Guaranteed uptime with real-time monitoring and auto-failover

functional_requirements.title: Secure print release mobile app
functional_requirements.description: Mobile app for releasing print jobs with authentication

functional_requirements.title: Advanced print workflow engine
functional_requirements.description: Conditional routing based on document content, user, department

non_functional_requirements.requirement: All tests must complete within 30 seconds
Database queries must have configurable timeout
non_functional_requirements.metric: Default query timeout = 30s, configurable via DB_QUERY_TIMEOUT env var

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Tests must not leave database locks or connections
non_functional_requirements.metric: Zero connection leaks, all tables truncated between tests

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Handler responses within timeout or return error

columns: (existing) - no schema change needed, query execution optimization required

risks.risk: Database connection pool exhaustion during test execution
risks.mitigation: Configure adequate MaxConns (5-10) for test environment, add connection lifecycle logging

risks.risk: Table lock contention from TRUNCATE TABLE operations
risks.mitigation: Use smaller batches for truncate, add timeout to truncate operations, consider transaction isolation level

risks.risk: Context propagation issues causing indefinite blocking
risks.mitigation: Always create derived context with timeout for DB ops, ensure parent context cancellation propagates

risks.risk: Test ordering dependencies causing state pollution
risks.mitigation: Each test should be independent, use t.Cleanup for guaranteed cleanup, run tests with -parallel flag

implementation_phases.name: Diagnosis and Logging
tasks: Add debug logging to ListControls handler
Add query execution time logging
Run failing test with verbose logging
Identify exact blocking point

implementation_phases.name: Add Timeout Contexts
tasks: Add context.WithTimeout to listControlsHandler
Update ListControls repository method to respect context timeout
Add timeout to all other database operations
Make timeout configurable via environment variable

implementation_phases.name: Fix Test Cleanup
tasks: Review setupHandlerTest cleanup
Add t.Cleanup for guaranteed resource cleanup
Ensure TruncateAllTables handles compliance tables
Add connection pool health check

implementation_phases.name: Optimize Queries
tasks: Review ListControls query for N+1 issues
Add appropriate indexes if needed
Ensure rows.Close() is called on all queries
Add connection pool metrics

implementation_phases.name: Verify and Validate
tasks: Run all compliance-service tests
Run with -race flag to detect race conditions
Run with -count=10 to check for flakiness
Verify no connection leaks in production code

key_gaps: Carbon footprint tracking and sustainability reporting
unique_selling_points: Modern microservices architecture vs competitors' legacy monolithic designs
Built-in multi-tenancy from ground up (competitors require enterprise editions)
Open source with potential for community contributions
Event-driven architecture using Redis for real-time processing
